message:
test(cma-es): pin invsqrt² == C⁻¹ + IPOP-restart Rastrigin convergence

Two follow-ups to 752a643's eigenvector layout fix:

1. **Regression test for the underlying bug**
   `tests::invsqrt_squared_equals_c_inverse` builds the literal
   covariance C = [[2, 1], [1, 3]] (whose analytic inverse is
   [[0.6, -0.2], [-0.2, 0.4]]), runs `update_eigensystem`, then
   computes A · A on the resulting `invsqrt` and asserts it matches
   C⁻¹ to 1e-9 per element. This pinpoints exactly the bug 752a643
   resolved: before the fix, this product had its off-diagonal
   signs flipped (because the row-major `idx` indexing was being
   applied to nalgebra's column-major eigenvector buffer, which
   silently produced `Uᵀ Λ⁻½ U` instead of the intended
   `U Λ⁻½ Uᵀ` = C⁻½). If the layout convention regresses again,
   this test fails on a 2×2 input — no need to reproduce the larger
   symptom.

2. **IPOP-style restarts for the Rastrigin convergence test**
   With the now-correct sampling, single-run CMA-ES on seed 42 lands
   in a Rastrigin local minimum (best f ≈ 5.97). Rastrigin is the
   canonical IPOP-CMA-ES benchmark anyway; added a
   `run_cma_with_restart` helper that doubles the population each
   restart and seeds each pass deterministically (42 + restart_idx),
   tracking the best-so-far across restarts. Test now passes
   reliably with 3 restarts and a 400k total-eval budget.

3. **Pre-existing `field_reassign_with_default` clippy errors**
   Four sites in the test module (run_cma, run_cma_with_restart,
   deterministic_seed_reproducible_full_model,
   respects_bounds_penalty_projection) used the
   `let mut x = Default::default(); x.field = …;` pattern that
   `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` flags. Rewrote them
   as struct-update syntax (`Options { …, ..Default::default() }`).
   No behavior change — same fields, same values. Unblocks any CI
   wired with `-D warnings`.

`cargo test`: 9/9 lib tests pass.
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean.
`cargo fmt --check`: clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
